response:
To find the **characteristic polynomial** of a matrix $ A $, we compute:

$$
\text{det}(A - \lambda I)
$$

where $ I $ is the identity matrix of the same size as $ A $, and $ \lambda $ is a variable.

---

### Step 1: Write the matrix $ A $

$$
A = \begin{pmatrix}
3 & -12 & 13 \\
-6 & 13 & 11 \\
-7 & -6 & -8
\end{pmatrix}
$$

---

### Step 2: Compute $ A - \lambda I $

$$
A - \lambda I = \begin{pmatrix}
3 - \lambda & -12 & 13 \\
-6 & 13 - \lambda & 11 \\
-7 & -6 & -8 - \lambda
\end{pmatrix}
$$

---

### Step 3: Compute the determinant of $ A - \lambda I $

$$
\det(A - \lambda I) = \begin{vmatrix}
3 - \lambda & -12 & 13 \\
-6 & 13 - \lambda & 11 \\
-7 & -6 & -8 - \lambda
\end{vmatrix}
$$

We will expand this determinant using cofactor expansion or row operations.

---

### Step 4: Use cofactor expansion

Let’s expand along the first row:

$$
\det(A - \lambda I) = (3 - \lambda) \cdot \begin{vmatrix}
13 - \lambda & 11 \\
-6 & -8 - \lambda
\end{vmatrix}
- (-12) \cdot \begin{vmatrix}
-6 & 11 \\
-7 & -8 - \lambda
\end{vmatrix}
+ 13 \cdot \begin{vmatrix}
-6 & 13 - \lambda \\
-7 & -6
\end{vmatrix}
$$

Now compute each 2x2 determinant:

1. $ \begin{vmatrix}
13 - \lambda & 11 \\
-6 & -8 - \lambda
\end{vmatrix} = (13 - \lambda)(-8 - \lambda) - (11)(-6) $

$$
= -104 - 13\lambda + 8\lambda
